basics.name: Luning Lin
basics.label: Ph.D. Candidate in Electronic Science and Technology
basics.summary: Ph.D. candidate at Zhejiang University researching integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) signal processing. Focused on designing efficient algorithms for sensing-aided communication enhancement and high-accuracy sensing based on communication signals.

location.address: College of Information Science & Electronic Engineering

work.name: University of Pisa
work.summary: Conducted research on ISAC systems under the co-advisory of Prof. Fulvio Gini and Prof. Maria Sabrina Greco.
highlights: Collaborated with the Radar and Sensor Systems Group
Developed novel algorithms for joint waveform design
Member of NESC Group at State Key Laboratory of Industrial Control Technology

awards.title: National Scholarship
awards.summary: The highest honor for graduate students in China, awarded to top-performing doctoral candidates.

awards.title: IEEE AESS Travel Grant
awards.awarder: IEEE Aerospace and Electronic Systems Society
awards.summary: The only Chinese student recipient that year. Supported attendance at IEEE Radar Conference.

awards.title: IEEE ComSoc Frontiers of Networking Student Competition Award
awards.summary: Recognized for outstanding research in the field of networking and communications.

publications.name: Sensing-Aided Precoding with High-Dynamic Moving Scatterers
publications.summary: Proposes a sensing-aided precoding scheme for high-dynamic environments with moving scatterers, enabling efficient communication enhancement through target localization.

publications.name: ISAC-Enabled Near-Field High-Mobility UAV Localization: Analysis, Bounds and Algorithm
publications.publisher: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
publications.summary: Investigates the near-field high-mobility UAV localization problem in ISAC systems, providing theoretical analysis, performance bounds, and an efficient localization algorithm.

publications.name: Joint Target Localization and Channel Estimation for ODDM-ISAC Systems
publications.summary: Addresses the joint target localization and channel estimation problem in orthogonal delay-Doppler multiplexing (ODDM) based ISAC systems.

projects.name: ISAC Waveform Design for High-Mobility Scenarios
projects.summary: Developing novel waveform designs that simultaneously support high-speed communication and accurate sensing in high-mobility environments such as UAV and vehicular networks.
highlights: Joint optimization of communication rate and sensing accuracy

projects.name: Near-Field ISAC for UAV Systems
projects.summary: Investigating near-field effects in ISAC systems for unmanned aerial vehicles, including channel modeling, performance analysis, and algorithm development.
highlights: Derivation of Cramér-Rao bounds for near-field localization
Development of efficient near-field beamforming algorithms
